volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Pot of Gold (Section 4.10.733 - Section 4.10.789b)

sections.label: Section 4.10.733
sections.original: Quo , obsecro , pacto esse possum ?
sections.translation: How , prithee , can I be so ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.733b
sections.original: Quia istuc facinus , quod tuom
sections.translation: Because that deed which is afflicting your mind , I did it , and I confess it .

sections.label: Section 4.10.734b
sections.original: Quid ego ex te audio ?
sections.translation: What is it I hear from you ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.735
sections.original: Id quod verumst .
sections.translation: That which is the truth .

sections.label: Section 4.10.735b
sections.original: Quid ego de te commerui , adulescens , mali ,
sections.translation: What evil , young man , have I deserved , by reason of which you should do thus , and go to ruin both me and my children ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.737
sections.original: Deus impulsor mihi fuit , is me ád illam inlexit .
sections.translation: A Divinity was my prompter ; he prompted me to do it [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.10.737b
sections.original: Quo modo ?
sections.translation: How ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.738
sections.original: Fateor peccavisse ét me culpam commeritum scio ;
sections.translation: I confess that I have done wrong , and I know that I deserve censure ; for that reason I ’ m come to beseech you , that , with feelings assuaged , you will pardon me .

sections.label: Section 4.10.740
sections.original: Cur id facere , ut id quod non tuom esset tangeres ?
sections.translation: Why did you dare do so , to touch that which was not your own ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.741
sections.original: Quid vis fieri ? factum est illud : fieri infectum non potest .
sections.translation: What do you wish to be done ? The thing has been done ; it can ’ t be undone . I believe that the Gods willed it , for if they hadn ’ t willed it , I know it wouldn ’ t have happened .

sections.label: Section 4.10.743
sections.original: At ego deos credo voluisse ut ápud me te in nervo enicem .
sections.translation: But I believe that the Gods have willed that I should be the death of you in fetters .

sections.label: Section 4.10.744
sections.original: Ne ístuc dixis .
sections.translation: Don ’ t say that !

sections.label: Section 4.10.744b
sections.original: Quid tibi ergo meam me invito tactiost ?
sections.translation: What business then have you to touch what is my own against my will ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.745
sections.original: Quia vini vitio atque amoris feci .
sections.translation: Because I did it under the evil influence of wine and love .

sections.label: Section 4.10.745b
sections.original: Homo audacissime ,
sections.translation: Most audacious man , that you should dare to come here to me with that speech , you impudent fellow ! For if this is lawful , so that you may be able to excuse it — let us openly , in broad daylight , plunder their golden trinkets from ladies — after that , if we are caught , let us excuse ourselves , that we did it when intoxicated ,

sections.label: Section 4.10.750
sections.original: nos fecisse amoris causa . nimis vilest vinum atque amor ,
sections.translation: by reason of being in love . Too cheap are wine and love , if one in liquor and in love is allowed to do with impunity whatever he pleases .

sections.label: Section 4.10.752
sections.original: Quin tibi ultro supplicatum venio ob stultitiam meam .
sections.translation: But I come to you of my own accord to supplicate you on account of my folly .

sections.label: Section 4.10.753
sections.original: Non mi homines placent qui quando male fecerunt purigant .
sections.translation: Persons don ’ t please me , who , when they ’ ve done wrong , excuse themselves . You knew that you had no right there ; you oughtn ’ t to have touched [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.10.755
sections.original: Ergo quia sum tangere ausus , haud causificor quin eam
sections.translation: Therefore , inasmuch as I did dare to touch , I make no objection to keep by all means .

sections.label: Section 4.10.756b
sections.original: Tun habeas me invito meam ?
sections.translation: You , keep what is my own against my will ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.757
sections.original: Haud te invito postulo ; sed meam esse oportere arbitror .
sections.translation: Against your will , I do not ask ; but I think that that which was yours ought to be mine [*] . Moreover , Euclio , you ’ ll find , I say , that mine it ought to be .

sections.label: Section 4.10.759
sections.original: Iam quidem hercle te ad praetorem rapiam et tibi scribam dicam ,
sections.translation: Now really , on my word , I ’ ll drag you to the Praetor and take proceedings [*] against you ,

sections.label: Section 4.10.760
sections.original: nisi refers .
sections.translation: unless you make restitution .

sections.label: Section 4.10.760b
sections.original: Quid tibi ego referam ?
sections.translation: Make restitution of what to you ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.760c
sections.original: Quod surripuisti meum .
sections.translation: What you ’ ve stolen of mine .

sections.label: Section 4.10.761
sections.original: Surripui ego tuom ? unde ? aut quid id est ?
sections.translation: I , stolen of yours ? Whence , or what is it ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.761b
sections.original: Ita te amabit Iuppiter ,
sections.translation: So shall Jupiter love you , how ignorant you are about it !

sections.label: Section 4.10.762b
sections.original: Nisi quidem tu mihi quid quaeras dixeris .
sections.translation: Unless , indeed , you tell me what you are enquiring for .

sections.label: Section 4.10.763
sections.original: Aulam auri , inquam , te reposco , quam tu mihi
sections.translation: The pot of gold , I say , I ’ m asking back of you , which you confessed to me that you had taken away .

sections.label: Section 4.10.764b
sections.original: Neque edepol ego dixi neque feci .
sections.translation: By my faith , I ’ ve neither said so , nor have I done it .

sections.label: Section 4.10.764c
sections.original: Negas ?
sections.translation: Do you deny it ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.765
sections.original: Pernego immo . nam neque ego aurum neque istaec aula quae siet
sections.translation: Yes , I do utterly deny it ; for neither the gold nor yet this pot , what it means , do I know or understand .

sections.label: Section 4.10.766b
sections.original: Illam , ex Silvani luco quam abstuleras , cedo .
sections.translation: Give me up that pot which you took away from the wood of Sylvanus . Come , give it me back ! I would rather give you the one-half of it . Although you are a thief to me , I ’ ll not be hard upon the thief . Give it me back .

sections.label: Section 4.10.769
sections.original: Sanus tu non es qui furem me voces . ego te , Euclio ,
sections.translation: You are not in your senses , to call me a thief ; I thought , Euclio , that you

sections.label: Section 4.10.770
sections.original: dé alia re rescivisse censui , quod ad me attinet ;
sections.translation: had come to the knowledge of another matter ; as concerns myself , it is a great matter which I wish to speak with you upon at your leisure , if you are at leisure .

sections.label: Section 4.10.772
sections.original: Dic bona fide : tu id aurum non surripuisti ?
sections.translation: Tell me , in good faith , have you not stolen that gold ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.772b
sections.original: Bona .
sections.translation: In good faith , No .

sections.label: Section 4.10.773
sections.original: Neque eum scis qui abstulerit ?
sections.translation: Nor know who has taken it away ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.773b
sections.original: Istuc quoque bona .
sections.translation: In good faith , No , to that as well .

sections.label: Section 4.10.773c
sections.original: Atque id si scies
sections.translation: But if you should know who has taken it away , will you discover it to me ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.774b
sections.original: Faciam .
sections.translation: I will do so .

sections.label: Section 4.10.774c
sections.original: Neque partem tibi
sections.translation: Nor accept of a share from him ,

sections.label: Section 4.10.775
sections.original: ab eo qui habet indipisces neque furem excipies ?
sections.translation: whoever he is , for yourself , nor harbour the thief ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.775b
sections.original: Ita .
sections.translation: Even so .

sections.label: Section 4.10.776
sections.original: Quid si fallis ?
sections.translation: What if you deceive me ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.776b
sections.original: Tum me faciat quod volt magnus Iuppiter .
sections.translation: Then may great Jupiter do unto me what he pleases .

sections.label: Section 4.10.777
sections.original: Sat habeo . age nunc loquere quid vis .
sections.translation: I ’ m satisfied . Come , then , say what you wish .

sections.label: Section 4.10.777b
sections.original: Si me novisti minus ,
sections.translation: If you know me but imperfectly , of what family I ’ m born : Megadorus here is my uncle ; Antimachus was my father ; my name is Lyconides ;

sections.label: Section 4.10.780
sections.original: mater est Eunomia .
sections.translation: Eunomia is my mother .

sections.label: Section 4.10.780b
sections.original: Novi genus . nunc quid vis ? id volo
sections.translation: I know the family ; now , what do you want ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.781b
sections.original: Filiam ex te tú habes .
sections.translation: I want to know this . You have a daughter of yours ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.781c
sections.original: Immo éccillam domi .
sections.translation: Why , yes , she ’ s there at home .

sections.label: Section 4.10.782
sections.original: Eam tu despondisti , opinor , meo avonculo .
sections.translation: You have , I think , recently betrothed her to my uncle ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.782b
sections.original: Omnem rem tenes .
sections.translation: You have the whole matter .

sections.label: Section 4.10.783
sections.original: Is me nunc renuntiare repudium iussit tibi .
sections.translation: He has now bade me announce to you his refusal of her [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.10.784
sections.original: Repudium rebus paratis , exornatis nuptiis ?
sections.translation: A refusal , when the things are got ready , and the wedding ’ s prepared ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.785
sections.original: ut illum di immortales omnes deaeque quantum est perduint ,
sections.translation: May all the immortal Gods and Goddesses confound him , so far as is possible , by reason of whom this day , unhappy wretch that I am , I have lost so much gold !

sections.label: Section 4.10.787
sections.original: Bono animo es , bene dice . nunc quae res tibi et gnatae tuae
sections.translation: Be of good heart , and speak in kindly terms ; now , a thing — may it turn out well and prosperously to you and your daughter .— May the Gods so grant — say .

sections.label: Section 4.10.789
sections.original: Ita di faciant .
sections.translation: May the Gods so grant .

sections.label: Section 4.10.789b
sections.original: Et mihi ita di faciant . audi nunciam .
sections.translation: And for me , too , may the Gods so grant it . Now , then , do you listen .
